# automq_connect_cluster

`automq_connect_cluster` manages a Kafka Connect Worker cluster.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "automq_connect_cluster" "example" {
  environment_id = var.automq_environment_id
  name           = "connect-cluster-prod"
  description    = "Production Connect worker cluster"

  plugins = [
    {
      name    = "s3-sink"
      version = "11.1.0"
    }
  ]

  kafka_cluster = {
    kafka_instance_id = automq_kafka_instance.example.id
  }

  capacity = {
    type = "provisioned"

    provisioned = {
      worker_resource_spec = "TIER2"
      worker_count         = 2
    }
  }

  compute = {
    type = "k8s"

    kubernetes = {
      cluster_id      = var.kubernetes_cluster_id
      namespace       = "connect"
      service_account = "connect-sa"
    }

    iam_role = var.connect_iam_role
  }

  worker_config = {
    "offset.flush.interval.ms" = "10000"
  }

  tags = {
    team = "data-platform"
  }
}

variable "automq_environment_id" {
  type = string
}

variable "kubernetes_cluster_id" {
  type = string
}

variable "connect_iam_role" {
  type = string
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `capacity` (Attributes) Worker capacity configuration for the Connect Cluster. Set `type` to `provisioned` for a fixed worker count or `autoscaling` for backend-managed scaling. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capacity))
- `compute` (Attributes) Compute backend where Connect workers run. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--compute))
- `environment_id` (String) AutoMQ environment ID that owns the Connect Cluster, for example `env-xxxxx`. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.
- `kafka_cluster` (Attributes) AutoMQ Kafka instance used by the Connect workers for internal Connect topics and worker coordination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--kafka_cluster))
- `name` (String) Connect Cluster name shown in AutoMQ. It must be 3 to 64 characters.
- `plugins` (Attributes List) Set of connector plugins installed into the worker cluster. Connectors on this cluster can only use connector classes provided by these plugins. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--plugins))

### Optional

- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description for the Connect Cluster.
- `metric_exporter` (Attributes) Metrics exporter configuration for Connect workers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--metric_exporter))
- `tags` (Map of String) User-defined tags for organizing Connect Clusters. AutoMQ may also return system tags.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))
- `version` (String) AutoMQ Connect worker version. If omitted, AutoMQ selects the backend default version.
- `worker_config` (Map of String) Worker-level Kafka Connect configuration overrides, such as converters or offset flush settings. Do not put plugin-specific connector configuration here; use `automq_connector.connector_config` instead.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) Connect Cluster ID assigned by AutoMQ, for example `connect-cluster-xxxxx`.
- `kafka_connect_version` (String) Kafka Connect framework version used by the worker runtime.
- `state` (String) Connect Cluster lifecycle state reported by AutoMQ, such as `RUNNING`, `CHANGING`, `FAILED`, or `DELETING`.
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp.

<a id="nestedatt--capacity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capacity`

Required:

- `type` (String) Capacity mode. Supported values are `provisioned` and `autoscaling`.

Optional:

- `autoscaling` (Attributes) Autoscaling capacity settings. Use this block when `type` is `autoscaling`; backend support depends on the target AutoMQ control plane. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling))
- `provisioned` (Attributes) Fixed capacity settings. Use this block when `type` is `provisioned`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capacity--provisioned))

<a id="nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capacity.autoscaling`

Required:

- `max_worker_count` (Number) Maximum number of Connect workers. Must be greater than or equal to `min_worker_count`.
- `min_worker_count` (Number) Minimum number of Connect workers. Must be at least 1.
- `worker_resource_spec` (String) Worker resource tier used by autoscaled workers. Supported values are `TIER1`, `TIER2`, `TIER3`, and `TIER4`.

Optional:

- `scale_in_policy` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling--scale_in_policy))
- `scale_out_policy` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling--scale_out_policy))

<a id="nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling--scale_in_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capacity.autoscaling.scale_in_policy`

Required:

- `cpu_utilization_percentage` (Number) Average CPU utilization threshold, from 1 to 100, below which AutoMQ may scale workers in.


<a id="nestedatt--capacity--autoscaling--scale_out_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capacity.autoscaling.scale_out_policy`

Required:

- `cpu_utilization_percentage` (Number) Average CPU utilization threshold, from 1 to 100, above which AutoMQ may scale workers out.



<a id="nestedatt--capacity--provisioned"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capacity.provisioned`

Required:

- `worker_count` (Number) Fixed number of Connect worker processes. Must be at least 1.
- `worker_resource_spec` (String) Worker resource tier. Supported values are `TIER1`, `TIER2`, `TIER3`, and `TIER4`.



<a id="nestedatt--compute"></a>
### Nested Schema for `compute`

Required:

- `type` (String) Compute type. Supported value is `k8s`; `k8s` requires the `kubernetes` block. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.

Optional:

- `iam_role` (String) Cloud IAM role used by Connect workers to access external services such as object storage. In AWS K8s deployments this is the IRSA role. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.
- `kubernetes` (Attributes) Kubernetes compute settings for worker pods. Required when `compute.type` is `k8s`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--compute--kubernetes))

<a id="nestedatt--compute--kubernetes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `compute.kubernetes`

Required:

- `cluster_id` (String) AutoMQ-registered Kubernetes cluster ID where worker pods are deployed. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.
- `namespace` (String) Kubernetes namespace for worker pods and related resources. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.
- `service_account` (String) Kubernetes ServiceAccount used by worker pods. In AWS environments this is typically bound to an IAM role by IRSA. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.

Optional:

- `scheduling_spec` (String) Optional Kubernetes scheduling YAML, such as node selectors, tolerations, or affinity rules. If omitted, AutoMQ keeps the backend default.



<a id="nestedatt--kafka_cluster"></a>
### Nested Schema for `kafka_cluster`

Required:

- `kafka_instance_id` (String) AutoMQ Kafka instance ID, for example `kf-xxxxx`. Changing it creates a new Connect Cluster.


<a id="nestedatt--plugins"></a>
### Nested Schema for `plugins`

Required:

- `name` (String) Plugin name registered in AutoMQ, such as `s3-sink` or `snowflake`.
- `version` (String) Plugin version registered in AutoMQ. The name and version pair must exist in the AutoMQ plugin repository.


<a id="nestedatt--metric_exporter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_exporter`

Optional:

- `remote_write` (Attributes) Prometheus Remote Write exporter configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--metric_exporter--remote_write))

<a id="nestedatt--metric_exporter--remote_write"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_exporter.remote_write`

Optional:

- `auth_type` (String) Authentication type for the remote write endpoint, such as basic, bearer token, or AWS SigV4 depending on backend support.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether remote write is enabled.
- `endpoint` (String) Prometheus Remote Write endpoint URL.
- `headers` (Map of String) Additional HTTP headers sent with remote write requests.
- `insecure_skip_verify` (Boolean) Whether to skip TLS certificate verification when sending metrics. Use only for controlled test environments.
- `labels` (Map of String) Additional labels attached to exported worker metrics.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Basic auth password.
- `prometheus_arn` (String) AWS Managed Service for Prometheus workspace ARN used with SigV4 authentication.
- `region` (String) AWS SigV4 region.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) Bearer token.
- `username` (String) Basic auth username.



<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import automq_connect_cluster.example env-xxxxx@connect-cluster-xxxxx
```

After the import completes, run `terraform plan` to review any drift in optional arguments.
